description_long: This object is a compact, vintage-style mechanical keyboard switch assembly, featuring a cluster of rectangular keys in beige and one prominent orange key. The keys are marked with simple symbols such as plus and minus signs, suggesting a specialized function, possibly for industrial or calculator use. The housing is metallic with visible screws and wiring, indicating a robust, utilitarian design. The underside reveals exposed mechanical switch components and soldered connections, typical of older electrical appliances. The keys appear to be made of plastic, while the frame and internal structure are metal, with some visible signs of age and wear. The overall construction is dense and functional, with no decorative elements.
description: A compact, vintage mechanical keyboard switch assembly with beige and orange keys marked by plus and minus symbols, set in a metal frame with visible wiring and mechanical components.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full set of keys, including the prominent orange key and the clear labeling of plus and minus symbols.
description_view_1: Top-down angled view highlighting the arrangement and labeling of the keys, with the orange key most visible.
description_view_2: Side view focusing on the profile of the keys and the exposed metal frame and wiring beneath the keycaps.
description_view_3: Rear-bottom view displaying the mechanical switch components, wiring, and mounting hardware underneath the keyboard.
